title: Git Version Control Basics
description: Learn standard commands for initializing, cloning, staging, committing, and branching in Git.

tasks.prompt: Initialize a new local Git repository in the current folder.
tasks.explanation: 'git init' sets up a hidden '.git' directory inside your directory, enabling tracking.

tasks.hint: Use git clone followed by the URL: 'git clone https://github.com/user/repo.git'
tasks.explanation: 'git clone' downloads a full copy of a remote Git repository along with all its version logs.

tasks.prompt: Check the status of tracked/untracked changes in the current branch.
tasks.explanation: 'git status' displays untracked, modified, or staged files compared to the last commit.

tasks.prompt: Stage all modified and untracked files in the current directory.
tasks.hint: Use git add followed by dot: 'git add .'
tasks.explanation: 'git add .' stages all directory changes, marking them as ready for the next commit.

tasks.prompt: Commit the staged changes with the description message 'initial commit'.
tasks.hint: Use commit command with message flag: 'git commit -m "initial commit"'
tasks.explanation: 'git commit -m' records the staged snapshot to history with a descriptive logs message.

tasks.prompt: Create and immediately switch to a new branch named 'feature'.
tasks.hint: Use git checkout with -b, or git switch with -c: 'git checkout -b feature'
tasks.explanation: Creates a new branch pointer and updates the HEAD directory pointer to the new branch.
